title: Design System: Aman Thanvi Personal Website

narrative.overview: A calm, precise instrument panel: cool near-neutral ink (dark) or paper (light) carrying one committed signal accent — a warm amber that reads as a beacon or status light. Field-tested and quietly distinctive, deliberately avoiding the hacker-HUD cliche the category defaults to. Expertise is communicated through sequencing, hierarchy, concise prose, and restraint; color is used sparingly and decisively.
keyCharacteristics: Two themes only: dark (default) and light, both meeting >=4.5:1 body contrast.
One signal accent across the whole page; no per-section color coding.
Archivo (display + UI) paired with JetBrains Mono for labels, metadata, and code.
Flat surfaces with 1px borders; elevation and accent appear on hover/focus.
Varied section compositions instead of one repeated card grid.

rules.name: The Single-Signal Rule
rules.body: One accent carries the brand; differentiate with type, weight, space, and rhythm rather than a second hue.

rules.name: The Accent-Budget Rule
rules.body: Accent is reserved for the name signature, primary CTA, links, active nav, list indices, and hover/focus. Tags stay neutral.

rules.name: The Visible-Without-JS Rule
rules.body: Scroll-reveal is an enhancement gated on a js class; content must render if JS never runs.

dos: Lead with verified signal: role, credentials, and contact before decoration.
Keep the accent scarce and deliberate.
Vary section layout for rhythm; one composition per idea.
Preserve light, dark, and reduced-motion behavior when changing components.
donts: Don't reintroduce multiple accent hues or per-section color coding.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, scanlines, or fake terminal chrome.
Don't gate content visibility on scroll-reveal.
Don't fill tags/chips with accent color.
